description: Renders an implicit curve defined by an equation f(x, y) = 0 on a 2D graph. Supports arbitrary algebraic curves including circles, ellipses, hyperbolas, and higher-order relations.
utility: Plot an implicit curve f(x, y) = 0 on a 2D graph

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph container to plot on

arguments.name: equation
arguments.description: Implicit equation as ASCII math string - e.g., "x^2 + y^2 - 25". The equation contains both x and y

alternates.description: Plot the implicit curve where f(x,y) = 0
Implicit curve with custom stroke color
Implicit curve written in equation form (auto-converted to f(x,y) = 0)

examples.expression:
G2 = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
implicitplot(G2, "x^2*y^2 - x^2 - y^2", c(red))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-6, 6), range(-6, 6), type(plotting))
implicitplot(G, "x^2 + y^2 - 25")

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
implicitplot(G, "x*y - 1")

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-4, 4), range(-4, 4), type(plotting))
a = 3
implicitplot(G, "(x^2 + y^2)^2 - a^2*(x^2 - y^2)", c(red))

G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-4, 4), range(-4, 4), type(plotting))
a = 3
implicitplot(G, "x^3 + y^3 - a*x*y")

examples.scenario: Plot x = f(y) curve using implicit form
examples.expression:
G = g2d(at(4,4), 20, 20, range(-5, 5), range(-5, 5), type(plotting))
implicitplot(G, "x = 2 + sin(y)", c(purple))
